report.title: Audit Report
report.aiFallbackTitle: Recommendations generated from rules — AI unavailable
report.aiFallbackDesc: Scores are accurate from page analysis. AI interpretation was skipped due to API limits or configuration. Retry later for AI-enhanced recommendations.
report.geoSub: Can generative engines read and recommend your product?

report.applySub: Implement the recommendations, then schedule a re-audit to measure your improvement. Pro users get automated monitoring.
report.conversionSummary: Strong price and imagery, but the description leans on features over benefits and the CTA is bland.
report.seoSummary: Clean Meta title and H1, but missing Product schema and long-tail keywords.
report.geoSummary: AI assistants read the page partially, but it lacks Q&A structure and a clear benefit statement.
report.trustSummary: Return policy and reviews are present. Missing visible shipping estimate and security badge.
report.rec1.problem: Description lists ingredients but never states the benefit the shopper will feel.
report.rec1.solution: Rewrite the first paragraph as a benefit-led hook: 'Wake up to softer, more hydrated skin in 7 nights.'
report.rec2.problem: ChatGPT reads the page but can't recommend it confidently — no 'who is this for' statement.
report.rec2.solution: Add a positioning line: 'For dry, sensitive skin seeking a natural, fragrance-free glow serum.'
report.rec3.problem: No Product schema markup. Google can't show rich results.
report.rec3.solution: Add JSON-LD Product schema with name, image, price, availability and rating.

blog.post10.title: AI Visibility for E-commerce: Complete GEO Playbook (2026)
blog.post10.excerpt: End-to-end guide to AI visibility — clarity, structure, trust, freshness, catalog rollout, monitoring, and GEO scoring for online stores.
blog.post.geo.p_1: Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) is the practice of optimizing your pages to appear in the answers of AI assistants like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews. As more shoppers rely on these tools to search for products, GEO has become a crucial factor in e-commerce.
blog.post.geo.h2_2: Why does GEO matter for your store?
blog.post.geo.p_2: According to a recent study, 27% of product searchers use AI assistants to get recommendations. If your product isn't readable and recommendable by these engines, you're losing a quarter of your potential audience.
blog.post.geo.h2_3: 5 steps to optimize your products' visibility in AI
blog.post.geo.p_3: AI assistants look for clear sentences that describe 'who this product is for'. Add a single sentence near the top of the page like: 'For dry, sensitive skin looking for a natural glow serum'.
blog.post.geo.p_4: AI engines prefer complete Q&A pair copies. Use 5 common questions with clear, direct answers.
blog.post.geo.p_5: Add Product schema and FAQ schema to help search engines and AI understand your page structure.
blog.post.geo.p_6: Language models handle clear, natural sentences better than exaggerated marketing phrases.
blog.post.geo.p_7: Use a tool like convaudit to measure your AI visibility score and discover needed improvements.
blog.post.geo.p_8: GEO isn't just a passing trend — it's the future of product search. Start optimizing your pages today to stay ahead of your competitors in the AI era.
blog.post.geo.imageAlt: Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) infographic — AI search visibility for e-commerce stores
blog.body.geoVsSeo.p_1: SEO optimizes for ranked links on Google and Bing. GEO optimizes for cited answers in ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, and Google AI Overviews. Both help shoppers find your products — through different surfaces.
blog.body.geoVsSeo.p_2: SEO focuses on keywords, backlinks, and crawlability. GEO focuses on clarity, structured facts, FAQs, and content that language models can quote accurately.
blog.body.geoVsSeo.h3_1: When SEO alone is not enough
blog.body.geoVsSeo.p_3: A page can rank #1 on Google but still be invisible to AI if product facts are buried in images, tabs, or vague marketing copy.
blog.body.geoVsSeo.p_4: Schema markup, clean headings, and fast pages help both. Strong SEO foundations make GEO easier because crawlers and LLMs read the same HTML.
blog.body.geoVsSeo.p_5: Run a combined audit: fix technical SEO, then add positioning statements, FAQs, and product schema so AI can recommend you with confidence.
blog.body.geoVsSeo.imageAlt: GEO vs SEO comparison chart — Google rankings vs AI assistant citations for e-commerce
blog.body.chatgpt.p_1: ChatGPT and similar assistants synthesize answers from page content they can parse: titles, bullet specs, FAQs, reviews summaries, and structured data. Opaque or image-only pages get skipped.
blog.body.chatgpt.p_2: Lead with who the product is for, list key specs in plain text, and answer the top five buyer questions on the page itself — not only in chat widgets.
blog.body.chatgpt.p_3: Include price range, sizing, materials, shipping regions, and return policy in visible HTML. Assistants use these to compare you against alternatives.
blog.body.chatgpt.p_4: JSON-LD helps models extract consistent facts. Pair Product schema with an FAQ block that mirrors real customer questions.
blog.body.chatgpt.p_5: Audit product URLs with a GEO score, fix low-trust signals, and re-test after content updates. Visibility in AI is iterative, not one-and-done.
blog.body.chatgpt.imageAlt: E-commerce store optimized for ChatGPT and AI shopping assistant recommendations
blog.body.checklist.p_1: Every product page should answer three questions in seconds: what is it, who is it for, and why buy from you. This checklist covers SEO and conversion basics.
blog.body.checklist.p_2: Unique title tag (50–60 chars), meta description with primary keyword, one H1 matching the product name, descriptive alt text on all images, and canonical URL set correctly.
blog.body.checklist.p_3: Use H2/H3 for benefits, specs, and care instructions. Include bullet lists for scanability. Avoid duplicate manufacturer copy across variants.
blog.body.checklist.p_4: Add Product schema with price, availability, SKU, and aggregateRating when applicable. Ensure mobile LCP under 2.5s and no critical content behind tabs only.
blog.body.checklist.p_5: Submit URL in Search Console, verify rich results, add internal links from category pages, and monitor rankings plus AI visibility monthly.
blog.body.checklist.imageAlt: Product page SEO checklist with title tags, meta descriptions, schema markup, and Core Web Vitals
blog.body.shopify.p_1: Shopify handles basics well — clean URLs, sitemaps, and mobile themes — but merchants still control titles, collections, and app bloat that can hurt performance.
blog.body.shopify.p_2: Build topical collections with unique descriptions (150+ words), logical hierarchy, and internal links to hero products. Avoid thin duplicate collection pages.
blog.body.shopify.p_3: Customize title tags via SEO apps or theme code. Add FAQ sections in the product template. Use Shopify's JSON-LD or augment with FAQ schema.
blog.body.shopify.p_4: Audit installed apps, defer non-critical scripts, compress images (WebP), and limit homepage carousels. Core Web Vitals affect both Google and user trust.
blog.body.shopify.p_5: Use Shopify Markets or translation apps with hreflang. Keep Arabic product copy in HTML, not image banners, so search engines and AI can read it.
blog.body.shopify.imageAlt: Shopify SEO guide — collections, product schema, speed optimization, and GEO for AI visibility
blog.body.woocommerce.p_1: Start with permalinks set to /%postname%/, install a reputable SEO plugin (Rank Math or Yoast), and enable XML sitemaps. WooCommerce exposes product schema when configured correctly.
blog.body.woocommerce.p_2: Write unique short and long descriptions. Use categories and tags with restraint — too many thin archives hurt crawl budget. Add breadcrumbs for structure.
blog.body.woocommerce.p_3: Enable product schema in your SEO plugin. Sync real reviews (Judge.me, Trustpilot) so aggregateRating appears in search. Fake reviews violate policies.
blog.body.woocommerce.p_4: Use object caching, a CDN, and limit heavy page builders on product templates. Slow TTFB hurts rankings in MENA markets where mobile share is high.
blog.body.woocommerce.p_5: Add an FAQ block per product, visible specs table, and Arabic/English parity if you sell cross-border. Audit with convaudit to score AI readability.
blog.body.woocommerce.imageAlt: WooCommerce SEO guide — WordPress store optimization, schema, and GEO setup
blog.body.salla.p_1: Salla powers thousands of Gulf stores with Arabic-first UX. SEO wins come from strong product titles, category descriptions, and trust pages — shipping, returns, and contact.
blog.body.salla.p_2: Target how Gulf shoppers search: dialect + product type + benefit (e.g. 'عباية كريب اسود مقاسات كبيرة'). Use those phrases in titles and first paragraph — not only in ads.
blog.body.salla.p_3: Custom domain connected, Google Search Console verified, meta title/description filled for homepage and categories, product images compressed, and Tamara/Tabby badges visible for trust.
blog.body.salla.p_4: Add structured FAQs on best sellers, keep specs in text not images, and link policies in the footer. Run GEO audits on top SKUs monthly.
blog.body.salla.imageAlt: Salla SEO guide for Saudi and Gulf e-commerce — Arabic keywords and AI visibility
blog.body.zid.p_1: Zid stores compete in crowded Saudi and GCC niches. Differentiate with detailed Arabic descriptions, size guides, and localized landing pages for campaigns.
blog.body.zid.p_2: One primary keyword per product page, unique meta descriptions, alt text in Arabic, and internal links from blog or lookbook content to products.
blog.body.zid.p_3: Connect custom domain, enable SSL, submit sitemap to Google, fix broken links after theme changes, and avoid duplicate product URLs from variants.
blog.body.zid.p_4: Publish comparison tables (you vs generic alternatives), answer 'مناسب لـ' questions in copy, and use convaudit to track AI visibility scores over time.
blog.body.zid.imageAlt: Zid SEO guide — organic traffic growth for Saudi and GCC e-commerce stores
blog.body.aiDesc.h2_1: Why AI helps — and where it fails
blog.body.aiDesc.p_1: AI drafts fast, consistent copy from specs and brand guidelines. It fails when you accept generic output without facts, tone edits, or locale-specific phrasing.
blog.body.aiDesc.p_2: Feed the model: product name, materials, dimensions, audience, objections, and banned phrases. Generate three variants, pick one, then human-edit the opening line and CTA.
blog.body.aiDesc.h3_1: SEO + GEO in the same pass
blog.body.aiDesc.p_3: Ask for a positioning sentence, spec bullets, FAQ pair, and meta description in one prompt. Plain language ranks and gets cited by assistants.
blog.body.aiDesc.p_4: Fact-check prices and claims, avoid hallucinated certifications, and A/B test on high-traffic SKUs. Never publish AI copy without a merchant review.
blog.body.aiDesc.p_5: Prioritize top 20% of revenue SKUs first, use templates for variants, and re-audit quarterly so descriptions stay aligned with inventory and policies.
blog.body.aiDesc.imageAlt: AI-powered product description writing workflow for SEO and GEO visibility
blog.body.aiVisibility.p_1: Shoppers ask AI for 'best moisturizer for humid climates' or 'trusted abaya store in Riyadh'. Stores that structure answers win citations — and clicks when users dig deeper.
blog.body.aiVisibility.p_2: Clarity (who/what/why), structure (headings, lists, schema), trust (reviews, policies, contact), and freshness (updated specs and stock). Weakness in any pillar lowers your GEO score.
blog.body.aiVisibility.p_3: Start with hero products, apply a standard template (positioning + specs + FAQ + schema), then batch-update long tail. Use crawl audits for full-site gaps.
blog.body.aiVisibility.p_4: Track GEO scores over time, set alerts when competitors improve, and re-run audits after theme or app changes that might hide text from crawlers.
blog.body.aiVisibility.p_5: Run a free convaudit scan on your top product URL, fix the top five recommendations, and expand to full-site crawl on Pro. AI visibility compounds like SEO — start early.
blog.body.aiVisibility.imageAlt: Four pillars of AI visibility for e-commerce — clarity, structure, trust, and freshness

concept.title: How convaudit turns a URL into revenue.
concept.subtitle: Watch the full workflow in 20 seconds — then run it on your own store.
concept.step1.title: 1. Paste your product URL
concept.step1.desc: Drop in any product page — Shopify, WooCommerce, Salla, Zid or custom. No setup, no snippet.
concept.step2.title: 2. AI reads the entire page
concept.step2.desc: Firecrawl extracts the title, description, images, price, CTAs, schema & policies. Gemini analyzes every element.
concept.step3.title: 3. Get your 4 scores + Store Score
concept.step3.desc: Conversion, SEO, GEO AI Visibility & Trust — combined into one number, benchmarked vs your competitor.

scores.title: One number. Four dimensions. Zero guesswork.
scores.subtitle: Your Store Score is a weighted blend of conversion, SEO, GEO visibility and trust. We plot you next to your competitor so you can see exactly where you win — and where you bleed.
scores.geo.title: Can AI recommend your product?
scores.geo.desc: Generative engines are the new search. We test whether ChatGPT, Perplexity and Google AI can parse — and would recommend — your page.
scores.geoNote: Your page is partially readable by AI assistants but lacks a clear positioning statement and structured Q&A — the two signals generative engines reuse when recommending products.
scores.leadSeo: You lead on SEO and Trust. Your competitor edges you on conversion. Fix your description and CTA to widen the gap to +18 pts.

compDemo.title: Not just scores. The exact fixes to close the gaps.
compDemo.subtitle: Every audit ends with prioritized, copy-ready recommendations — problem, solution, and projected impact. No more guessing what to change.
compDemo.rec1.problem: The description lists ingredients but never states the benefit the shopper feels.
compDemo.rec1.solution: Rewrite the opening as a benefit-led hook: 'Wake up to visibly smoother, hydrated skin in 7 nights.' Lead with the outcome, then prove it.
compDemo.rec2.problem: ChatGPT can read the page but can't confidently recommend it — no clear 'who is this for'.
compDemo.rec2.solution: Add a positioning line: 'For dry and sensitive skin seeking a natural, fragrance-free glow serum.' AI models reuse these exact sentences.

testimonials.title: Real stores. Real revenue. Real results.
testimonials.quote1: convaudit found three schema gaps Google Search Console never flagged. Two weeks after fixing them, our organic clicks jumped 34%. Paid for itself instantly.
testimonials.quote2: The GEO score was a wake-up call. We rewrote our product positioning and now Perplexity actually cites our store when people ask about natural skincare.
testimonials.quote3: We audit every new product before launch. The AI-generated descriptions alone save my team 6 hours a week — and they convert better than our old copy.
testimonials.quote4: Benchmarking against our main competitor changed our roadmap. We finally knew exactly where we were losing and could prioritize fixes that moved the needle.
testimonials.quote5: As an affiliate, I audit merchant pages before promoting. convaudit tells me in a minute whether a page will actually convert my traffic. Game changer.
faq.title: Questions, answered.
faq.q1: How does the AI audit actually work?
faq.a1: Paste a product URL and we use Firecrawl to read the full rendered page — title, description, images, price, CTAs, policies, schema and structure. Gemini then scores it across four dimensions (conversion, SEO, GEO visibility, trust), benchmarks it against your competitor if provided, and generates prioritized recommendations plus ready-to-paste copy.
faq.q2: What is GEO / AI Visibility scoring?
faq.a2: GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) measures whether AI assistants like ChatGPT, Perplexity and Google AI Overviews can parse your page and would recommend your product. We analyze how quotable, structured and clearly positioned your content is — the same signals generative engines use to cite sources.
faq.a3: Any public product page works — Shopify, WooCommerce, Salla, Zid, Magento, Wix, custom-built stores and affiliate landing pages. Because we read the rendered HTML, no platform integration or tracking snippet is required.
faq.a4: We use Kashier for secure payment processing in Egyptian Pounds (EGP). Kashier supports bank cards, Vodafone Cash, and InstaPay — with full PCI compliance.
faq.q5: What do I get in the free plan?
faq.a5: Three audits per month with your overall Store Score, a report summary, and history of your last 3 reports. No credit card required. Upgrade to Pro for full analysis, AI recommendations, and 30 audits per month.
faq.q6: Can the AI Generator write my product copy?
faq.a6: Yes. On Pro and above, every audit includes AI-generated alternatives for your product title, description, a 5-question FAQ, meta description, and ad copy for Meta, TikTok and Google — all tailored to your product and optimized for both SEO and GEO.
faq.q7: Is my data and competitor data private?
faq.a7: Completely. Audits are tied to your account and never shared. We don't store the crawled page content beyond generating your report, and we never alert competitors that they've been benchmarked.

planCompare.title: Everything included, side by side
planCompare.fullAnalysis: Full SEO, GEO, CRO & Trust analysis
pricingFaq.title: Pricing questions
pricingFaq.a1: Yes. The Free plan includes 3 audits per month, a basic overall score, and history of your last 3 reports — no credit card required.
pricingFaq.q2: What's the difference between Pro and Business?
pricingFaq.a2: Pro includes 30 audits/month, full analysis, Quick Fixes, Content Improver, Customer Eye Test, 1 competitor comparison, PDF export, and weekly reports. Business adds unlimited audits, full website crawl, Watchdog 24/7, up to 3 stores, white-label PDFs, and automatic monitoring.
pricingFaq.a3: Yearly plans are billed once per year at 1,490 EGP (Pro) or 3,990 EGP (Business) — save versus paying monthly (199 EGP/mo Pro, 499 EGP/mo Business).
pricingFaq.q4: What payment methods do you accept?
pricingFaq.a4: All payments are processed securely through Kashier in EGP. We accept bank cards, Vodafone Cash, and InstaPay.
pricingFaq.q5: What happens when I hit my audit limit?
pricingFaq.a5: Audit limits are enforced on the server. When you reach your monthly limit, you'll need to wait until the next billing cycle or upgrade your plan.
pricingFaq.a6: Yes. You can cancel your subscription at any time. Your plan remains active until the end of the current billing period.
